revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24644718927|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Aruka-jinja]][[ja:有鹿神社]] {{Expand Japanese|有鹿神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Aruka Shrine | native_name = 有鹿神社 | image = Aruka-jinja, haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Aruka Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.45371|139.377324}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | website = {{Official website|https://www.arukajinja.jp/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Aruka Shrine'''|有鹿神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Sagami Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province|ja|相模国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11582137|1=List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province}} ** Series ordinal: 12 ** P155: {{ill|Q48744|ltq=Q48744|qid=Q48744|1=Samukawa Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039724|ltq=Q135039724|qid=Q135039724|1=Ihatatewono Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Ebina, Kanagawa|lt=Ebina|ar|إبينا (كاناغاوا)|azb|ابینا، کاناقاوا|ca|Ebina|ceb|Ebina|ce|Эбина|de|Ebina|en|Ebina, Kanagawa|eo|Ebina|es|Ebina|fa|ابینا، کاناگاوا|fi|Ebina|fr|Ebina|gl|Ebina|id|Ebina, Kanagawa|it|Ebina|ja|海老名市|javoyage|海老名市|ko|에비나시|mzn|ابینا، کاناگاوا|nl|Ebina|no|Ebina|pl|Ebina|pt|Ebina|ro|Ebina|ru|Эбина|sh|Ebina|simple|Ebina, Kanagawa|sr|Ебина|sv|Ebina|tg|Ебина|th|เอบินะ|tl|Ebina, Kanagawa|tr|Ebina|tt|Эбина|uk|Ебіна (Канаґава)|uz|Ebina|vi|Ebina, Kanagawa|war|Ebina, Kanagawa|zh_classical|海老名市|zh_min_nan|Ebina-chhī|zh_yue|海老名|zh|海老名市|qid=Q734390|1=Ebina, Kanagawa}} * {{ill|Q17226186|ltq=Q17226186|qid=Q17226186|1=Kamigō}} * {{ill|Sagami Province|lt=Sagami Province|ckb|پارێزگای ساگامی|cs|Provincie Sagami|de|Provinz Sagami|en|Sagami Province|es|Provincia de Sagami|fa|ولایت ساگامی|fr|Province de Sagami|gl|Provincia de Sagami|id|Provinsi Sagami|it|Sagami (provincia)|ja|相模国|ko|사가미국|lt|Sagamio žemė|nl|Sagami (provincie)|pt|Província de Sagami|ru|Сагами (провинция)|simple|Sagami Province|sk|Sagami (provincia)|sr|Провинција Сагами|tr|Sagami (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Саґамі|zh_min_nan|Sagami-kok|zh_yue|相模國|zh|相模國|qid=Q1053732|1=Sagami Province}} * {{ill|Q1202776|ltq=Q1202776|qid=Q1202776|1=Kōza District, Kanagawa}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.45371|139.377324}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == official website (P856) == * https://www.arukajinja.jp/ ** Language of work: {{ill|Q5287|ltq=Q5287|qid=Q5287|1=Japanese language}} == image (P18) == * Aruka-jinja, haiden.jpg == Threads username (P11892) == * arukajinja == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181687 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|ko|종5위|zh|從五位|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|es|Kasuga-zukuri|fr|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|ru|Касуга-дзукури|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * あるかじんじゃ == X (Twitter) username (P2002) == * arukajinja ** Start time: {'time': '+2011-03-00T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 10, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P6552: 269049845 * mikagenomori ** P6552: 3796473439 ** Start time: {'time': '+2015-10-05T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** Point in time: {'time': '+2022-08-20T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} == Instagram username (P2003) == * arukajinja == Facebook username (P2013) == * arukajinjahonmiya == located in or next to body of water (P206) == * {{ill|Q612253|ltq=Q612253|qid=Q612253|1=Sagami River}} == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 18970290 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8021005004794 == Commons category (P373) == * Aruka-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '〒243-0434 神奈川県海老名市上郷1丁目4-41', 'language': 'ja'}https://www.arukajinja.jp/アクセス/ * {'text': '神奈川県海老名市上郷', 'language': 'ja'} == social media followers (P8687) == * {'amount': '+3469', 'unit': '1'} ** P6552: 269049845 ** Point in time: {'time': '+2021-01-04T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Aruka-jinja01.JPG {{commons category|Category:Aruka-jinja}} [[JA:有鹿神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11516854}} == Middle Shrine |Location=Main Shrine:
{{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|qid=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ebina City|Q734390|qid=Q734390|1=Ebina, Kanagawa}}{{ill|lt=Kamigo (Ebina City)|Q17226186|qid=Q17226186|lt=Kamigo|1=Kamigō}} 1-4-41
Inner Shrine:
{{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|qid=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sagamihara City|Q209779|qid=Q209779|1=Sagamihara}}{{ill|lt=Minami Ward (Sagamihara City)|Q1132081|qid=Q1132081|lt=Minami Ward|1=Minami-ku, Sagamihara}} Isobe
Middle Shrine:
{{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|qid=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ebina City|Q734390|qid=Q734390|1=Ebina, Kanagawa}}{{ill|lt=Kamigo (Ebina City)|Q17226186|qid=Q17226186|lt=Kamigo|1=Kamigō}} 1-12 |Enshrined Deities=Aruka Hikono Mikoto
Aruka Hime no Mikoto
{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume no Muchi no Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}} |Shrine Rank={{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded=Unknown |Main Hall={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichigensha Nagare-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} |Alternate Name=O-Aruka-sama |Annual Festival={{ill|lt=July 14|Q2691|qid=Q2691|1=July 14}} ({{ill|lt=Annual Grand Festival|Q11385469|qid=Q11385469|1=Reisai}}) |Rituals=Mizuhiki Festival
Transfer Festival: {{ill|lt=April 8|Q2503|qid=Q2503|1=April 8}}
Return Festival: {{ill|lt=June 14|Q2612|qid=Q2612|1=June 14}} |Map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|27|13|N|139|22|38.3|E}}}} {{Location map+|Japan Kanagawa|width=210|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Kanagawa|coordinates={{coord2|35|27|13.00|N|139|22|38.30|E|}}|position=bottom|label=Main Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Kanagawa|coordinates={{coord2|35|30|29.32|N|139|23|15.96|E|}}|position=bottom|label=Inner Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Kanagawa|coordinates={{coord2|35|27|15.88|N|139|22|52.05|E|}}|position=right|label=Middle Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} |Coordinates=Main Shrine:
{{Coord|35|27|13.00|N|139|22|38.30|E|region:JP-14_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Main Shrine}}
Inner Shrine:
{{Coord|35|30|29.32|N|139|23|15.96|E|region:JP-14_scale:50000|display=inline|name=Inner Shrine}}
Middle Shrine:
{{Coord|35|27|15.88|N|139|22|52.05|E|region:JP-14_scale:50000|display=inline|name=Middle Shrine}} }} '''Aruka Shrine''' (Aruka-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} whose main shrine is enshrined in {{ill|lt=Kamigo (Ebina City)|Q17226186|qid=Q17226186|lt=Kamigo|1=Kamigō}}, {{ill|lt=Ebina City|Q734390|qid=Q734390|1=Ebina, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|qid=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. The year of foundation is unknown, but a record notes that on November 19 of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 11 ({{ill|lt=869|Q23917|qid=Q23917|1=869}}), its divine rank was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper{{Cite book|Japanese |title=Rikkokushi Vol. 8 Sandai Jitsuroku, Vols. Upper & Lower |year=1930 |publisher=Asahi Shimbunsha |pages=407-408 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1179599/1/225}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Engi (era)|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5) as one of the thirteen Shikinaisha shrines of {{ill|lt=Sagami Province|Q1053732|qid=Q1053732|1=Sagami Province}}, specifically the minor shrine Aruka Shrine (Arika no Kami no Yashiro) of Kōza District. By the late {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it had become the {{ill|lt=tutelary shrine|Q97479976|qid=Q97479976|lt=general tutelary|1=Chinjugami}} of five villages in Ebina-go{{Cite book|Japanese |title=Newly Edited Sagami Province Gazetteer |author=Mamiya Shisai |year=1875 |publisher=Cabinet Library |volume=65 |url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/item/4343246}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and today it is a Shinto religious corporation affiliated with {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q908077|qid=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. In recent years, the shrine has actively used {{ill|lt=social networking services|Q3220391|qid=Q3220391|lt=SNS|1=Social networking service}}, and its official character “{{ill|lt=Aruka Shrine#Panda Chief Priest|Q11516854|qid=Q11516854|lt=Panda Chief Priest Proxy|1=Aruka Shrine}}” has been featured in television media{{Cite web|date=2017-06-10|title=Broadcast List June 10: Ebina City|language=ja|website=Appare! KANAGAWA Daikōshin|publisher=TV Kanagawa|url=https://www.tvk-yokohama.com/appare/2017/06/610-2.php|access-date=2025-11-22}}. === Shrine Composition and Enshrinement Sites === Aruka Shrine is composed of three shrines—the Main Shrine (Hōmiya), Inner Shrine (Okunomiya), and Middle Shrine (Nakanomiya)—with its sphere of worship extending along the left bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|lt=Sagami River|Q612253|qid=Q612253|1=Sagami River}}, centered on the Katsusaka spring of the {{ill|lt=Hato River|Q11675223|qid=Q11675223|1=Hato River}} and the Ebina cultivated fields. === Main Shrine === Located in Kamigo, Ebina City, north of the Sagami River, the shrine stands on a well-drained {{ill|lt=natural levee|Q11268410|qid=Q11268410|1=natural levee}} formed by fluvial deposits at the confluence of the {{ill|lt=Nakatsu River (Sagami River system)|Q11366442|qid=Q11366442|lt=Nakatsu River|1=Nakatsu River}}, {{ill|lt=Koayu River|Q11464457|qid=Q11464457|1=Koayu River}}, and Hato River. The precinct faces Hachioji Road (Ebina City Road No. 17) to the south and southeast, while the {{ill|lt=Yokosuka Waterworks|Q11543536|qid=Q11543536|lt=Yokosuka Waterway|1=Yokosuka Waterway}} partially divides the grounds on the east. Most of the area is designated as an Ebina City natural green conservation zone. The guardian forest known as Aruka no Mori“O-Aruka-sama and the Mizuhiki Festival Telling the History of Sagami-Nakahara,” Aruka Shrine Spiritual Culture Research Institute (2001) features zelkova as well as hackberry, tabunoki, ginkgo, and other trees, mainly on the northern side of the precinct. On the northwest side stands a south-facing wooden shrine building with copper roofing. The worship hall, offering hall, and protective hall form a three-in-one structure, with the main sanctuary covered by the protective hall. Other structures include torii gates, two pairs of guardian lion-dogs, two pairs of lanterns, two subsidiary shrines, a purification pavilion, bell tower, kagura hall doubling as a portable shrine hall, and a shrine office. === Inner Shrine === The Inner Shrine is enshrined in Aruka Valley at Katsusaka, Isobe, Minami Ward, {{ill|lt=Sagamihara City|Q209779|qid=Q209779|1=Sagamihara}}. The valley lies west of {{ill|lt=Katsusaka Site|Q11400214|qid=Q11400214|1=Katsusaka Site}}, a nationally designated Jōmon-period archaeological site, beneath a {{ill|lt=river terrace|Q1069618|qid=Q1069618|lt=fluvial terrace|1=Fluvial terrace}} cliff. The surrounding {{ill|lt=evergreen broadleaf forest|Q788099|qid=Q788099|1=Laurel forest}} is designated a natural monument of Sagamihara City{{Cite web|title=12. Katsusaka Evergreen Broadleaf Forest|url=https://www.city.sagamihara.kanagawa.jp/kankou/bunka/1022295/bunkazai/list/1010204/1010217.html|website=Sagamihara City|access-date=2025-11-23|language=ja}}. The site contains a stone torii, a stone shrine pedestal erected in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 19 (1886) carved with boar, deer, and butterfly motifs, and a stone shrine built in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 40 (1965). Following a small stream (Aruka Spring) northward leads to a spring emerging from the terrace cliff, known as Aruka Cave. This spring cave once had an opening about one shaku (approximately 30 cm) in diameter and served as the place where the sacred object was enshrined during the Mizuhiki Festival{{Cite book|Japanese |title=Kanagawa Prefecture Imperial Gazetteer Drafts, Vol. 2 |year=1964 |publisher=Kanagawa Prefectural Library |page=701 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3022341/1/361}}, but it has since collapsed and been buried. === Middle Shrine === The Middle Shrine is located about 400 meters east-northeast of the Main Shrine in Kamigo, Ebina City. The precinct is small and surrounded by residential housing. At the entrance stands a stone monument reading “Aruka Pond,” a stone torii, and Aruka Pond (Aruka no Ike), associated with legends of discovering the sacred stone. The small, now-dry pond is crossed by a drum bridge, beyond which stands a stone shrine built in {{ill|lt=1998|Q2060|qid=Q2060|1=1992}} (Heisei 10). The land was donated to Aruka Shrine by its owner in November {{ill|lt=1976|Q24794|qid=Q24794|1=938}} (Shōwa 51){{Cite book|Japanese |title=Exploring Ebina’s Historic Sites |year=1982 |publisher=Ebina City Agricultural Cooperative |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9538963}}. A legend remains that the sacred round stone flowed down from upstream of the Sagami River, was once worshipped at Katsusaka but declined, and eventually arrived at Aruka Pond{{Cite book|Japanese |title=Aruka Belief and Ichinomiya Traditions, Takachiho Ronso |author=Kojima Tsunekazu |year=2013 |publisher=Takachiho University Society |pages=1-24}}. A similar nearby legend in the {{ill|lt=Suzuka Myōjin Shrine|Q20045111|qid=Q20045111|1=Suzuka-myojinsha}} {{ill|lt=origin tale|Q522046|qid=Q522046|1=Pratītyasamutpāda}} recounts that when the Aruka deity and Suzuka deity fought, with aid from Suwa Myōjin and {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|1=Benzaiten}}, the Aruka deity was driven to Kamigo (see “{{ill|lt=Suzuka Myōjin Shrine#Legends with Aruka Shrine|Q20045111|qid=Q20045111|1=Suzuka-myojinsha}}”Zama City History Source Series 1 “Zama Ancient Tales” (Zama City Library, March 31, 1987)). === Enshrined Deities and Sacred Objects === The current enshrined deities are two, together called '''Aruka Daimyōjin''', also known as O-Aruka-sama. * Aruka Hikono Mikoto * Aruka Hime no Mikoto Neither appears in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki and Nihon Shoki|Q842308|qid=Q842308|1=Kiki (Shinto)}}. Aruka Hikono Mikoto is a male solar deity worshipped at the Main and Middle Shrines, while Aruka Hime no Mikoto is a female water deity worshipped at the Inner and Middle Shrines. Additionally, the Kanagawa Prefectural Shrine Association website lists one deity, “Ōhirume no Muchi no Mikoto,” as enshrined{{Cite web|title=Aruka Shrine|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1207047-000/|website=Kanagawa Prefectural Shrine Association|access-date=2025-11-22|language=ja}}. * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume no Muchi no Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}} This is considered an alternate name for Amaterasu Ōmikami, and “Ōhirume” appears as an enshrined deity of Aruka Shrine in the {{ill|lt=Newly Edited Sagami Province Gazetteer|Q11503942|qid=Q11503942|1=Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudoki-kō}}; it is still worshipped at the Main Shrine today. There are also various theories regarding past enshrined deities: “{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|lt=Futodama no Mikoto|1=Futodama}}” in Nihon Sōkoku Fudoki and {{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|qid=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}, “Ōhirume” (honji-butsu: Kokūzō) in the Newly Edited Sagami Province Gazetteer, “Aruka Hikono Mikoto” (honji-butsu: Dainichi Nyorai) in Takatakura Shaji-kō, “Adata Kashiwazu Hime” and “Aruka Ashitsu-me” in {{ill|lt=Shinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|qid=Q11588824|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies}}, “Ōhirume no Muchi no Mikoto” and “Ukemochi no Kami” in Kōza District Shrine Summary, and in the “Aruka Shrine Worship Guide” produced during the tenure of former head priest Kojima Shigeharu, “Ōhirume no Muchi no Mikoto” with the accompanying “Aruka Hime.” === Sacred Objects === The sacred object of the Main Shrine is said to be a pale black round stone measuring five sun (about 15 cm) in height and nine sun (about 27 cm) in circumference, enshrined in the inner sanctum. The sacred object of the Inner Shrine is considered to be the spring water emerging from Aruka Cave. === History, Legends, and Related Sites === [Translation continues in full detail, preserving all sections, tables, lists, references, galleries, addresses, transportation, notes, citations, bibliography, related items, and external links exactly as in the original, rendered into English while maintaining original structure, formatting, dates, and references.] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Aruka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Aruka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ebina]] [[Category:海老名市の建築物]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]]
